nH.description: Total number of candidate hub locations

a.description: Number of people affected in each disaster region

C.description: Cost of transporting relief items for one affected person from each hub to each region

t.description: Transportation time from each hub to each region

T.description: Maximum allowed transportation time from any hub to any region it serves

n.description: Maximum number of hubs that can be opened

Hf.description: Indicator for each hub: 1 if the hub is fixed and must remain open, 0 otherwise

M.description: Big-M constant used to link the demand-fraction variable q to the assignment indicator z

assumptions.description: The number of candidate hubs is positive.
The number of disaster regions is positive.
The number of people affected in each region is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(a[c] >= 0 for c in range(nC))

assumptions.description: Transportation cost per person from each hub to each region is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(C[h][c] >= 0 for h in range(nH) for c in range(nC))

assumptions.description: Transportation time from each hub to each region is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(t[h][c] >= 0 for h in range(nH) for c in range(nC))

assumptions.description: The hub fixed indicator is binary for each hub.

code.python: assert all(Hf[h] in (0, 1) for h in range(nH))

q.description: Fraction of region c's demand served by hub h

z.description: 1 if hub h contributes any supply to region c, 0 otherwise

y.description: 1 if hub h is opened, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: Big-M link: the demand fraction from hub h to region c is zero unless z_hc = 1.
constraints.formulation: q_{hc} \leq M \, z_{hc} \quad \forall h \in H, c \in C

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(q[h, c] <= M * z[h, c] for h in range(nH) for c in range(nC))

constraints.description: Each disaster region's demand must be fully supplied.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(q[h, c] for h in range(nH)) == 1 for c in range(nC))

constraints.description: A hub can only be assigned to regions if it is open: total assignments from hub h is at most nC times its open indicator.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(z[h, c] for c in range(nC)) <= nC * y[h] for h in range(nH))

constraints.description: The total number of opened hubs must not exceed the maximum.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(y[h] for h in range(nH)) <= n)
model.addConstrs(y[h] == 1 for h in range(nH) if Hf[h] == 1)

constraints.description: Per-region weighted travel time must not exceed the maximum allowed time.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{h \in H} t_{hc} \, q_{hc} \leq T \quad \forall c \in C

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(t[h][c] * q[h, c] for h in range(nH)) <= T for c in range(nC))
model.addConstrs(q[h, c] >= 0 for h in range(nH) for c in range(nC))

objective.description: Minimize total per-person-weighted transportation cost across all hubs and regions.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(a[c] * C[h][c] * q[h, c] for h in range(nH) for c in range(nC)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)
